decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.16°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed because it exceeded the maximum bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its physical limit in this model, the next most effective lever is to increase the geometric cross-section of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat distribution. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.84 to ~1.008), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain width of 1.2.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing the local thermal gradient and peak temperature. This is the primary remaining degree of freedom since spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Increased heat flux towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the domain edges (x=0 and x=1.2); further increases may be limited by geometry bounds.
If the temperature reduction is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to adjust the base plate conductivity or spreader height.
Ensure the new width does not cause overlap issues if other components were to move (currently static).
